title: acp::run_prompt_loop hard-codes max_iterations and prompt builder ignores cwd/mode
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/acp.rs:777-988 (run_prompt_loop)
## Violation
The agentic loop is a 211-line function with several intertwined design defects:

```rust
let max_iterations = 50; // Safety limit
...
let system_prompt = crate::prompt::build_system_prompt(None, None, None);
...
let mut all_messages: Vec<crate::proxy::ChatMessage> =
    vec![crate::proxy::ChatMessage { role: "system".to_string(), ... }];
```

1. `max_iterations = 50` is a magic local, not configurable, and silently caps real work.
2. `build_system_prompt(None, None, None)` discards hook instructions, custom instructions, and memory.
3. The function builds a fresh `reqwest::Client::new()` per call (line 779) instead of holding a pooled client on the server struct.
4. The function clones `self.config.active_provider().thinking` every iteration inside the 50-iteration loop.
5. `serde_json::from_value(tools.clone()).unwrap_or_default()` (line 813) swallows tool-definition parse errors and silently strips the tool array.

Cyclomatic complexity by inspection is well above the 15 threshold.
## Mandated refactor
Split `run_prompt_loop` into (a) `build_request`, (b) `dispatch`, (c) `consume_stream`. Hoist the `reqwest::Client` to a field of `AcpServer`. Pull `max_iterations` and per-call timeout onto `AcpConfig`. Replace `unwrap_or_default()` on tool definitions with an explicit `?`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
